focal_character.name: Fatou Ndiaye
focal_character.occupation: junior clerk at a district land registry office
focal_character.ordinary_life_state: Fatou returns to a backlog of twenty-three unresolved boundary-dispute files at the Dakar registry and, instead of starting with the oldest as told, asks what the outcome actually needs before opening any file.

problem_structure.unknown: the category of evidence that would resolve the boundary dispute, not a person, date, or amount of land
data: a chamber of pigeonholes holding files, ledgers, stamped forms, and rolled maps
a dull, unstamped envelope in a slot on the left wall
the stacks and arrangements left behind by the three previous solvers
problem_structure.condition: the ruling turns on testimony type — oral boundary acknowledgment, pre-1987, admissible under Article 12(b) — rather than on the documents themselves

gate_details.correct_approach: Fatou stood at the threshold with her back half-turned to the data and asked what kind of answer the room wanted, recalling a precedent that hinged on evidence type rather than documents. Naming the category silently made the unstamped envelope brighten; she opened it and placed its contents on the table, and the gate closed.

new_texture: Morning sun slants through the high windows of the Dakar District Land Registry office.
Boundary-dispute case backlogs pile up on junior clerks' desks, with supervisors instructing them to start with the oldest file.
A colleague shuffling papers and another arguing with a petitioner over a missing signature both go still or change register when a plain question is asked aloud.

anchor_update.observation:
Grade 1–2 / executor / room emphasises sequential file order; gap: unstamped envelope (slot L7); Reg 1 throughout; closure attempt min 10 — failed
Grade 1–2 / system builder / room mirrors categorical grid; gap: categorisation ignores evidentiary type; Reg flicker 1→3→1; closure attempt min 14 — failed
Grade 1–2 / information gatherer / room yields complete audit; gap: no question asked of data; Reg 1 undisturbed; closure attempt min 18 — failed
Grade 1–2 / correct / Reg 2 at threshold-standing (min 19:03); Reg 3 at right question (min 19:47); gate closed min 20
anchor_update.reader_can_suspect_update: The anchor's notation now explicitly confirms that naming the category of an unknown precedes closure even when unspoken, and tracks whether solvers orient toward the type of answer wanted rather than its content.

macro_mystery_update.evidence: The anchor's notebook logs all three wrong-approach solvers' entries side by side within the same gate event, each with precise register timestamps, before recording the correct approach — extending the simultaneous-approaches pattern first seen in chapter 004's trace.
